Migraine and cardiovascular disease: sys.text: CONCLUSION Migraine is associated with a twofold increased risk of ischaemic stroke, which is only apparent among people who have migraine with aura.

Migraine and cardiovascular disease: systematic review and meta-analysis.
Migraine and cardiovascular disease: sys.text: Only one study investigated the association between women who had migraine with aura and myocardial infarction and death due to cardiovascular disease, showing a twofold increased risk.

Migraine and cardiovascular disease: sys.title: Migraine and cardiovascular disease: systematic review and meta-analysis.
Migraine and cardiovascular disease: sys.text: Additional analyses indicated a significantly higher risk among people who had migraine with aura (2.16, 1.53 to 3.03) compared with people who had migraine without aura (1.23, 0.90 to 1.69; meta-regression for aura status P=0.02).

Migraine and cardiovascular disease: sys.title: Migraine and cardiovascular disease: systematic review and meta-analysis.

Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.title: Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-vessel vasculitis
Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.text: Here we show that chromatin fibers, so-called neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs), are released by ANCA-stimulated neutrophils and contain the targeted autoantigens proteinase-3 (PR3) and myeloperoxidase (MPO).

Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.title: Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-vessel vasculitis
Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.text: Deposition of NETs in inflamed kidneys and circulating MPO-DNA complexes suggest that NET formation triggers vasculitis and promotes the autoimmune response against neutrophil components in individuals with SVV.

Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.title: Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-vessel vasculitis
Netting neutrophils in autoimmune small-.text: Small-vessel vasculitis (SVV) is a chronic autoinflammatory condition linked to antineutrophil cytoplasm autoantibodies (ANCAs).

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.title: The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not Required for Derivation of Pluripotent Stem Cells
The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: Thus, although Blimp1 is obligatory for PGC specification, it is not required for the reversion of epiSCs to ESCs and for their maintenance thereafter.

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.title: The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not Required for Derivation of Pluripotent Stem Cells
The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: By using a genetic approach, we demonstrate that Blimp1 is dispensable for the derivation and maintenance of ESCs and postimplantation epiblast stem cells (epiSCs).

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.title: The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not Required for Derivation of Pluripotent Stem Cells
The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: Whereas Prdm14 alone can promote reprogramming and is important for the propagation of the pluripotent state, it is not known whether Blimp1 is similarly involved.

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: Notably, Blimp1 is also dispensable for reprogramming epiSCs to ESCs.

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: Blimp1 (Prdm1), the key determinant of primordial germ cells (PGCs), plays a combinatorial role with Prdm14 during PGC specification from postimplantation epiblast cells.

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: They together initiate epigenetic reprogramming in early germ cells toward an underlying pluripotent state, which is equivalent to embryonic stem cells (ESCs).

The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.title: The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not Required for Derivation of Pluripotent Stem Cells
The Germ Cell Determinant Blimp1 Is Not.text: This study suggests that reprogramming, including that of somatic cells to ESCs, may not entail an obligatory route through a Blimp1-positive PGC-like state.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.title: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: Antiretroviral therapy has substantial potential to prevent HIV-associated tuberculosis.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: CONCLUSIONS Antiretroviral therapy is strongly associated with a reduction in the incidence of tuberculosis across all CD4 count strata.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.title: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: We conducted a systematic review of studies that analysed the impact of antiretroviral therapy on the incidence of tuberculosis in adults with HIV infection.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.title: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: Randomised controlled trials, prospective cohort studies, and retrospective cohort studies were included if they compared tuberculosis incidence by antiretroviral therapy status in HIV-infected adults for a median of over 6 mo in developing countries.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: Antiretroviral therapy has substantial potential to prevent HIV-associated tuberculosis.

Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.title: Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of Tuberculosis in Adults with HIV: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Antiretroviral Therapy for Prevention of.text: Antiretroviral therapy is strongly associated with a reduction in the incidence of tuberculosis in all baseline CD4 count categories: (1) less than 200 cells/µl (hazard ratio [HR] 0.16, 95% confidence interval [CI] 0.07 to 0.36), (2) 200 to 350 cells/µl (HR 0.34, 95% CI 0.19 to 0.60), (3) greater than 350 cells/µl (HR 0.43, 95% CI 0.30 to 0.63), and (4) any CD4 count (HR 0.35, 95% CI 0.28 to 0.44).

Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.title: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.text: Here, we found that Protein Kinase G1 (PKG-I) localized presynaptically in nociceptor terminals plays an essential role in the expression of spinal LTP.

Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.title: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.text: Synaptic long-term potentiation (LTP) at spinal neurons directly communicating pain-specific inputs from the periphery to the brain has been proposed to serve as a trigger for pain hypersensitivity in pathological states.

Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.title: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.text: Finally, behavioural analyses in vivo showed marked defects in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice in several models of activity-induced nociceptive hypersensitivity, and pharmacological studies identified a clear contribution of PKG-I expressed in spinal terminals of nociceptors.

Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.title: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.text: Inositol 1,4,5-triphosphate receptor 1 and myosin light chain kinase were recruited as key phosphorylation targets of presynaptic PKG-I in nociceptive neurons.

Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.title: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity
Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dep.text: Analyses of synaptic failure rates and paired-pulse ratios indicated a role for presynaptic PKG-I in regulating the probability of neurotransmitter release.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: OBJECTIVE To obtain summary estimates of the accuracy of a single baseline measurement of the Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay (Roche Diagnostics) for the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in patients presenting to the emergency department.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: STUDY SELECTION Studies were included if they evaluated the diagnostic accuracy of a single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in patients presenting to the emergency department with suspected acute coronary syndrome.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: CONCLUSIONS The results indicate that a single baseline measurement of the Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay could be used to rule out acute myocardial infarction if lower cut-off values such as 3 ng/L or 5 ng/L are used.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: DESIGN Systematic review and meta-analysis of diagnostic test accuracy studies.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: However, this method should be part of a comprehensive triage strategy and may not be appropriate for patients who present less than three hours after symptom onset.

Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.title: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline m.text: This means that if 21 of 100 consecutive patients have the target condition (21%, the median prevalence across the studies), 2 (95% confidence interval 2 to 3) of 21 patients with acute myocardial infarction will be missed (false negatives) if 14 ng/L is used as a cut-off value and 18 (13 to 25) of 79 patients without acute myocardial infarction will test positive (false positives).

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: We studied the heterogeneity in the age-specific associations of clinically measured blood pressure with 12 acute and chronic cardiovascular diseases, and estimated the lifetime risks (up to 95 years of age) and cardiovascular disease-free life-years lost adjusted for other risk factors at index ages 30, 60, and 80 years.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: In each age group, the lowest risk for cardiovascular disease was in people with systolic blood pressure of 90-114 mm Hg and diastolic blood pressure of 60-74 mm Hg, with no evidence of a J-shaped increased risk at lower blood pressures.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: METHODS We used linked electronic health records from 1997 to 2010 in the CALIBER (CArdiovascular research using LInked Bespoke studies and Electronic health Records) programme to assemble a cohort of 1·25 million patients, 30 years of age or older and initially free from cardiovascular disease, a fifth of whom received blood pressure-lowering treatments.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: INTERPRETATION The widely held assumptions that blood pressure has strong associations with the occurrence of all cardiovascular diseases across a wide age range, and that diastolic and systolic associations are concordant, are not supported by the findings of this high-resolution study.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: BACKGROUND The associations of blood pressure with the different manifestations of incident cardiovascular disease in a contemporary population have not been compared.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: We studied the heterogeneity in the age-specific associations of clinically measured blood pressure with 12 acute and chronic cardiovascular diseases, and estimated the lifetime risks (up to 95 years of age) and cardiovascular disease-free life-years lost adjusted for other risk factors at index ages 30, 60, and 80 years.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: METHODS We used linked electronic health records from 1997 to 2010 in the CALIBER (CArdiovascular research using LInked Bespoke studies and Electronic health Records) programme to assemble a cohort of 1·25 million patients, 30 years of age or older and initially free from cardiovascular disease, a fifth of whom received blood pressure-lowering treatments.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: In each age group, the lowest risk for cardiovascular disease was in people with systolic blood pressure of 90-114 mm Hg and diastolic blood pressure of 60-74 mm Hg, with no evidence of a J-shaped increased risk at lower blood pressures.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: Pulse pressure associations were inverse for abdominal aortic aneurysm (HR per 10 mm Hg 0·91 [95% CI 0·86-0·98]) and strongest for peripheral arterial disease (1·23 [1·20-1·27]).

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: Despite modern treatments, the lifetime burden of hypertension is substantial.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: In each age group, the lowest risk for cardiovascular disease was in people with systolic blood pressure of 90-114 mm Hg and diastolic blood pressure of 60-74 mm Hg, with no evidence of a J-shaped increased risk at lower blood pressures.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: Compared with diastolic blood pressure, raised systolic blood pressure had a greater effect on angina, myocardial infarction, and peripheral arterial disease, whereas raised diastolic blood pressure had a greater effect on abdominal aortic aneurysm than did raised systolic pressure.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: METHODS We used linked electronic health records from 1997 to 2010 in the CALIBER (CArdiovascular research using LInked Bespoke studies and Electronic health Records) programme to assemble a cohort of 1·25 million patients, 30 years of age or older and initially free from cardiovascular disease, a fifth of whom received blood pressure-lowering treatments.

Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.title: Blood pressure and incidence of twelve cardiovascular diseases: lifetime risks, healthy life-years lost, and age-specific associations in 1·25 million people
Blood pressure and incidence of twelve c.text: INTERPRETATION The widely held assumptions that blood pressure has strong associations with the occurrence of all cardiovascular diseases across a wide age range, and that diastolic and systolic associations are concordant, are not supported by the findings of this high-resolution study.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.title: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis
KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: Previous studies investigating the role of smooth muscle cells (SMCs) and macrophages in the pathogenesis of atherosclerosis have provided controversial results owing to the use of unreliable methods for clearly identifying each of these cell types.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.title: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis
KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: Our findings indicate that the contribution of SMCs to atherosclerotic plaques has been greatly underestimated, and that KLF4-dependent transitions in SMC phenotype are critical in lesion pathogenesis.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.title: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis
KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: SMC-specific conditional knockout of Krüppel-like factor 4 (Klf4) resulted in reduced numbers of SMC-derived MSC- and macrophage-like cells, a marked reduction in lesion size, and increases in multiple indices of plaque stability, including an increase in fibrous cap thickness as compared to wild-type controls.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.title: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis
KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: On the basis of in vivo KLF4 chromatin immunoprecipitation–sequencing (ChIP-seq) analyses and studies of cholesterol-treated cultured SMCs, we identified >800 KLF4 target genes, including many that regulate pro-inflammatory responses of SMCs.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.title: KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of smooth muscle cells has a key role in atherosclerotic plaque pathogenesis
KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: Here, using Myh11-CreERT2 ROSA floxed STOP eYFP Apoe−/− mice to perform SMC lineage tracing, we find that traditional methods for detecting SMCs based on immunostaining for SMC markers fail to detect >80% of SMC-derived cells within advanced atherosclerotic lesions.

KLF4-dependent phenotypic modulation of.text: These unidentified SMC-derived cells exhibit phenotypes of other cell lineages, including macrophages and mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs).

A culture-independent sequence-based met.title: A culture-independent sequence-based metagenomics approach to the investigation of an outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli O104:H4.
A culture-independent sequence-based met.text: In phase 1, a de novo assembly approach was developed to obtain a draft genome of the outbreak strain.

A culture-independent sequence-based met.title: A culture-independent sequence-based metagenomics approach to the investigation of an outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli O104:H4.
A culture-independent sequence-based met.text: CONCLUSIONS AND RELEVANCE These results suggest the potential of metagenomics as a culture-independent approach for the identification of bacterial pathogens during an outbreak of diarrheal disease.

A culture-independent sequence-based met.title: A culture-independent sequence-based metagenomics approach to the investigation of an outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli O104:H4.
A culture-independent sequence-based met.text: DESIGN, SETTING, AND PATIENTS In a retrospective investigation, 45 samples were selected from fecal specimens obtained from patients with diarrhea during the 2011 outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli (STEC) O104:H4 in Germany.

A culture-independent sequence-based met.title: A culture-independent sequence-based metagenomics approach to the investigation of an outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli O104:H4.
A culture-independent sequence-based met.text: MAIN OUTCOMES AND MEASURES The recovery of genome sequence data for the purposes of identification and characterization of the outbreak strain and other pathogens from fecal samples.

A culture-independent sequence-based met.title: A culture-independent sequence-based metagenomics approach to the investigation of an outbreak of Shiga-toxigenic Escherichia coli O104:H4.
A culture-independent sequence-based met.text: OBJECTIVE To explore the potential of metagenomics, which is the direct sequencing of DNA extracted from microbiologically complex samples, as an open-ended clinical discovery platform capable of identifying and characterizing bacterial strains from an outbreak without laboratory culture.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: Stimulation with glucose causes WFS1 translocation from the ER to the plasma membrane, where it forms a complex with adenylyl cyclase 8 (AC8), an essential cAMP-generating enzyme in the β-cell that integrates glucose and GLP-1 signalling.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: Here we provide evidence that WFS1 is essential for glucose- and glucagon-like peptide 1 (GLP-1)-stimulated cyclic AMP production and regulation of insulin biosynthesis and secretion.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: Wolfram syndrome 1 (WFS1) has been shown to be an important regulator of the ER stress signalling pathway; however, its role in β-cell function remains unclear.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: The reduction of WFS1 protein on the plasma membrane during ER stress is a contributing factor for β-cell dysfunction and progression of type 2 diabetes.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: These findings reveal that an ER-stress-related protein has a distinct role outside the ER regulating both insulin biosynthesis and secretion.

Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.title: Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase 8 interact at the plasma membrane to regulate insulin production and secretion
Wolfram syndrome 1 and adenylyl cyclase.text: Endoplasmic reticulum (ER) stress causes pancreatic β-cell dysfunction and contributes to β-cell loss and the progression of type 2 diabetes.
